channel.title: GeoPulse. Geopolitics in brief
channel.description: Signal-first briefings on geopolitics, edited by Lavkesh Dwivedi.

item.title: U.S. Is Said to Be Sending Kushner and Witkoff to Negotiations in Pakistan
item.description: The Israeli military extended its truce with Lebanon by three weeks, despite striking Hezbollah targets on Friday. The cease-fire aims to end the war in Iran. The Israeli military said it had struck Hezbollah targets on Friday, but its truce with Lebanon appeared to be holding.

item.title: Top Iran diplomat set to travel to Pakistan for talks on ceasefire with US
item.description: The talks come amid ongoing tensions between Iran and the US. Live updates: Iran's top diplomat is expected to travel to Pakistan by this weekend for talks about a ceasefire with the US AP News.

item.title: Iran’s top diplomat heads to Pakistan as fresh talks expected, sources say
item.description: Iran's top diplomat is heading to Pakistan for fresh talks, sources say. Live updates: Iran’s top diplomat heads to Pakistan as fresh talks expected, sources say CNN.

item.title: Israel-Lebanon ceasefire extended by three weeks
item.description: Israel and Lebanon extended their ceasefire by three weeks after US-brokered talks. U. S. President Donald Trump announces the development after meeting representatives from both countries in Washington. Israel-Lebanon ceasefire extended by three weeks.

item.title: Turkish foreign minister says Israel has become ‘direct threat to global security,’ urges
item.description: Turkey's foreign minister called Israel a direct threat to global security, urging a collective response. Turkish foreign minister says Israel has become ‘direct threat to global security,’ urges 'collective response' Anadolu Ajansı.

item.title: U.S. and Iran remain locked in Strait of Hormuz standoff, Israel launches new strikes in Lebanon
item.description: The US and Iran remain in a standoff in the Strait of Hormuz, with Hezbollah rejecting a Lebanon ceasefire. Live Updates: U. S. and Iran remain locked in Strait of Hormuz standoff, Hezbollah rejects Lebanon ceasefire CBS News.

item.title: Hegseth warns Iran that US forces will ‘shoot to destroy’ any ships laying mines in Strait of Hormuz
item.description: US forces will shoot to destroy any Iranian ships laying mines in the Strait of Hormuz. Hegseth warns Iran that US forces will ‘shoot to destroy’ any ships laying mines in Strait of Hormuz Fox News.

item.title: Pentagon email floats suspending Spain from NATO, other steps over Iran rift, source says
item.description: A Pentagon email suggested suspending Spain from NATO due to a rift over Iran, a source said. Exclusive: Pentagon email floats suspending Spain from NATO, other steps over Iran rift, source says Reuters.

item.title: No 10 says Falklands sovereignty rests with UK after report of US 'review'
item.description: The UK government insists Falklands sovereignty rests with the UK after a Pentagon report suggested the US might reconsider its position in response to the UK's refusal to join the Iran war.

item.title: US has three aircraft carriers in the Middle East for first time since 2003
item.description: The US has deployed three aircraft carriers in the Middle East for the first time since 2003, as part of a blockade on Iran's ports, amid a fragile truce and escalating tensions.

item.title: PM Sanchez rebuffs US idea of suspending Spain from NATO
item.description: Spanish Prime Minister Sanchez rejected a reported US plan to suspend Spain from NATO over its opposition to the war on Iran, stating the country's position remains unchanged. PM Sanchez responded to reports that the US may suspend Madrid from NATO over its opposition to the war on Iran.

item.title: Is the U.S. Trying to Suspend Spain From NATO? Sánchez Addresses Reported Pentagon Email
item.description: Spanish Prime Minister Sanchez denied a reported US plan to suspend Spain from NATO, saying the country's position on the Iran war remains firm and unchanged. Is the U. S. Trying to Suspend Spain From NATO?

item.title: Iranian FM Abbas Araghchi to reach Pakistan tonight for talks with U.S.: report
item.description: Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi is set to arrive in Pakistan tonight for talks with the US, with a US delegation likely to follow on April 25 to discuss a framework for negotiations.

item.title: UN says Israeli strikes in Lebanon, Hezbollah rockets into Israel may breach international law
item.description: Israeli strikes in Lebanon and Hezbollah rockets into Israel may breach international law according to the UN. UN says Israeli strikes in Lebanon, Hezbollah rockets into Israel may breach international law Reuters.

item.title: How Iran is wielding the Strait of Hormuz as leverage
item.description: Iran is using the Strait of Hormuz as leverage. The US wants to negotiate. While the US is signaling a desire to negotiate, Iranians are playing for time. counting on the world's dependence on Persian Gulf energy exports and their strongest weapon, the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: Iran-Iraq Tanker War redux? Why the Strait of Hormuz crisis is different
item.description: The Strait of Hormuz crisis is different from the 1980s Tanker War. The Tanker War between Iran and Iraq in the 1980s also disrupted global shipping. But the situation today is different.

item.title: Israel’s Benjamin Netanyahu says he received treatment for prostate cancer
item.description: Israel's Benjamin Netanyahu received radiation therapy for early-stage prostate cancer. Netanyahu's medical report said the Israeli leader was treated with radiation therapy for early-stage prostate cancer. Israel’s Benjamin Netanyahu says he received treatment for prostate cancer.

item.title: Sally Rooney, Greta Thunberg back Palestine Action before court date
item.description: Over 130 public figures including Sally Rooney and Greta Thunberg support Palestine Action. More than 130 public figures express support for the proscribed group as the UK prepares for another legal fight.

item.title: US Justice Department to close investigation into Fed Chair Powell, top federal prosecutor says
item.description: The US Justice Department closed its investigation into Fed Chair Powell. The investigation's closure removes one more obstacle to the confirmation of Kevin Warsh, President Donald Trump's pick to lead the Federal Reserve.

item.title: Putin may attend G20 summit in Miami after U.S. invitation: Kremlin
item.description: Putin may attend the G20 summit in Miami after a US invitation. The Russian President has not attended a G20 summit since 2019; Trump has adopted a more friendly attitude towards Russia in his second term in office.
